title: App stuck on Loading: freezePrototype breaks pdf-lib import
description: tauri.conf.json freezePrototype:true (m0-9) froze Object.prototype; pdf-lib throws at import ('Cannot assign to read only property toString') in strict-mode ESM. save.ts imports pdf-lib at the top of main.ts's graph, so the whole module aborted -> static Loading..., no handlers. Surfaced in M1 (pdf-lib added); CI missed it (freeze only applies in the live webview). Fix: freezePrototype:false + canary test.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 1902d06: freezePrototype set false; subprocess canary guards regression; full gate green (55 tests). App module no longer aborts at import.

title: Scaffold the SignetPDF project (Tauri 2 + Vite + TypeScript)
description:
Create the project skeleton for SignetPDF: a Tauri 2 shell with a Vite + TypeScript frontend. No UI framework; the DOM is driven directly. Bake the name in from this first commit.
Naming:
- Product name: SignetPDF.
- npm package + repo name: "signetpdf".
- Tauri bundle identifier: "org.signetpdf" (adjust if you register a domain).
- Window title / app display name: "SignetPDF".
TypeScript:
- tsconfig with "strict": true (plus noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes). The model and coordinate types are real interfaces, not JSDoc.
- Vite handles TS transpile; type-checking is a separate 'tsc --noEmit' step (see m0-2), because Vite/esbuild does not type-check.
Acceptance:
- "npm run tauri dev" opens a window titled SignetPDF showing the frontend.
- package.json "name" is "signetpdf"; productName/identifier set in tauri.conf.json.
- Rust side compiles; layout: /src (TS frontend), /src-tauri (Rust).
- 'tsc --noEmit' passes on the skeleton.
Commit: "chore: scaffold signetpdf (tauri 2 + vite + typescript)".
notes: Verified headlessly: npm run typecheck, npm run build (tsc+vite), and cargo build all pass. GUI window launch (npm run tauri dev) not exercised in this environment — confirm visually; the window title/size live in src-tauri/tauri.conf.json. Bumped vite to 8 (esbuild advisory; 0 audit vulns).
close_reason: Scaffold complete and builds end-to-end (TS typecheck, vite build, cargo build). Named SignetPDF/org.signetpdf throughout.

title: Integrate PDF.js and render a bundled fixture (worker path)
description:
Add pdfjs-dist; render a bundled fixture. CRITICAL: ensure the PDF.js worker is bundled and its path resolves inside the Tauri webview. Sort now, not later.
Test first: Vitest spec loads the fixture and asserts page count.
Acceptance: fixture renders; no worker errors in the webview.
Commit: "feat(render): integrate pdf.js with bundled worker".
close_reason: pdf.js integrated with the worker bundled via Vite ?url; loader+render modules added; page-count test green. Build emits the worker as a standalone asset.

title: Define DocumentModel shape and createModel factory
description:
Single source of truth. Real TypeScript interfaces (no JSDoc), all fields readonly so the compiler enforces immutability.
Model: { sourceBytes: Uint8Array, fieldValues: FieldValue[], annotations: Annotation[], pages: PageGeometry[], dirty: boolean }.
FieldValue { kind:'field', fieldName: string, value: string | boolean }.
Annotation = TextBox { kind:'text', id, page, origin:{x,y}, width, height, text, fontSize } | SignatureStamp { kind:'signature', id, page, origin, width, height, pngBytes } — a discriminated union on 'kind'.
ALL geometry is PDF user space (points, bottom-left origin) — never screen pixels. Brand the user-space Point type (e.g. a 'UserSpace' tag) so a screen-pixel point cannot be passed where a user-space point is expected.
Test first: createModel(bytes) returns an empty, non-dirty model holding the bytes; types reject mutation of a readonly field.
Commit: "feat(model): add document model and factory".
close_reason: DocumentModel + createModel added; readonly interfaces, branded UserSpacePoint, frozen empty model. Compile-time + runtime immutability tested. Gate green.

title: Coordinate helper modelToScreen (the seam)
description:
THE critical maths, one module, one home (DRY). modelToScreen(point, pageGeometry, viewport): user-space bottom-left -> screen px top-left, honouring scale and rotation (0/90/180/270).
Test first: table-driven known inputs -> known screen coords per rotation.
Commit: "feat(coords): add modelToScreen conversion".
design:
Two correctness traps the seam must own:
1. HiDPI: the canvas backing store is sized by devicePixelRatio (DPR). The seam must factor DPR into the screen<->user-space conversion, or overlays drift on Retina/fractional-scaling displays. Take DPR as part of 'viewport' and test at DPR 1, 2 and 1.5.
2. PDF.js vs pdf-lib agreement: PDF.js viewports bake in /Rotate; pdf-lib draws in unrotated user space. Decide ONE canonical space the model stores (recommend: unrotated user space, the pdf-lib draw space) and make modelToScreen the only place rotation is applied. The save projection (m1-7) must read coordinates in exactly that same space — a mismatch here is the classic 'annotation is right on screen, wrong in the saved file' bug.
notes: DPR decision: handled in the renderer (canvas backing store), NOT in the seam. The seam works in CSS px so overlays and the CSS-displayed canvas share one space; this supersedes the earlier 'DPR in viewport' note.
close_reason: modelToScreen added in one module; replicates pdf.js viewport transform (cross-checked against convertToViewportPoint for rot 0 and 90); branded ScreenPoint; table-driven tests for all rotations. Gate green (44 tests).

title: Coordinate helper screenToModel + round-trip property
description:
screenToModel = exact inverse of modelToScreen, same module.
Test first: property test screenToModel(modelToScreen(p)) ~= p across all rotations and several scales.
Commit: "feat(coords): add screenToModel inverse with round-trip tests".
close_reason: screenToModel exact inverse added (same module); round-trip property test green across rotations/scales/grid. Gate green (46 tests).

title: Save projection: model -> pdf-lib -> bytes (empty round-trip)
description:
Save side of the seam. Load sourceBytes with pdf-lib, apply nothing for an empty model, return bytes. Pure, no DOM.
Test first (golden-file): empty-model save re-opens with identical page count and AcroForm field set, for each non-XFA fixture.
Commit: "feat(save): add save projection with empty-model round-trip".
close_reason: Pure saveModel (model->pdf-lib->bytes) added; empty-model golden round-trip preserves page count + AcroForm field set across all non-XFA fixtures. Gate green (51 tests).

title: Detect AcroForm fields; refuse XFA gracefully
description:
Enumerate AcroForm fields (name, type, page, rect) via PDF.js/pdf-lib. Detect XFA and refuse with a clear user message (no attempt to render).
Test first: AcroForm fixture yields the expected field list with types; XFA fixture triggers refusal.
Commit: "feat(forms): detect acroform fields and refuse xfa".
close_reason: hasXfa (pdf-lib /XFA) + XFA open-refusal wired; listFormFields (pdf.js) enumerates all field types with name/kind/page/rect/options, per-widget. 6 tests; gate green (61).

title: Save projection writes field values via pdf-lib
description:
Extend the save projection: apply model.fieldValues through pdf-lib getForm().
Test first (golden-file): fill every field type, save, re-open -> values persist exactly.
Commit: "feat(save): write acroform field values".
notes: Appearance streams: after setting field values, call form.updateFieldAppearances() (or set NeedAppearances) so values render in viewers that do not regenerate appearances (Preview.app, many browsers). Golden-file test must assert the value is visible, not just present in the field dictionary — extract text from the rendered page, not only the AcroForm value.
close_reason: saveModel writes all field types via pdf-lib (text/checkbox/choice/radio with name-or-index mapping) + updateFieldAppearances. Golden round-trip: fill all types -> save -> reopen -> values persist. Gate green (74).

title: Text tool: click to create a text box
description:
A tool where clicking the page creates a TextBox; convert the click via screenToModel and add through addAnnotation.
Test first: a click at a known screen point yields a TextBox whose origin is the expected user-space coordinate.
Commit: "feat(text): add click-to-create text box".
close_reason: Pure createTextBoxAt: screen click -> screenToModel -> addAnnotation; 2 tests

title: Save projection draws text boxes via pdf-lib
description:
Extend the save projection: draw each TextBox with pdf-lib drawText at the correct page, position and size, using the embedded font.
Test first (golden-file): a box at a known user-space coord produces extractable text at the expected position.
Commit: "feat(save): draw free-text annotations".
close_reason: saveModel draws TextBoxes via pdf-lib drawText with embedded Noto subset; golden round-trip asserts content + baseline position; fontBytes injected via SaveOptions

title: Signature pad: draw and export transparent PNG
description:
A canvas drawing surface that exports a transparent-background PNG (Uint8Array).
Test first: output begins with the PNG signature and is non-empty after a stroke.
Commit: "feat(sign): add signature drawing pad".
close_reason: createSignaturePad (pointer drawing, transparent canvas, empty/clear state) + pure dataUrlToBytes; 5 tests (PNG signature decode + DOM state/export with stubbed context). node-canvas avoided to keep CI matrix light.

title: Save projection embeds signature via pdf-lib
description:
Extend the save projection: embedPng + drawImage at the stamp's page, position and size.
Test first (golden-file): output contains an image XObject on the expected page at the expected box.
Commit: "feat(save): embed signature image".
close_reason: saveModel embeds PNG stamps via embedPng+drawImage at page/origin/size; golden test reconstructs the operator-list CTM (save/restore stack) to assert page + box. 1 test.

title: Packaging and distribution for downloadable releases
description: 0.4.0 ships from source only; GitHub releases are source tags with no binaries, and there is no signing/notarization. This epic covers producing installable, trust-passing builds for macOS, Windows and Linux and attaching them to releases. Children: release build workflow, macOS notarization, Windows code-signing.

title: Release build workflow: build Tauri bundles per-OS and attach to the GitHub release
description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that, on a version tag (v*), builds the Tauri bundle on the ubuntu/macos/windows matrix and uploads the installers (.dmg/.app, .msi/.exe, .deb/.AppImage) as assets on the matching GitHub release. Today releases are source-only tags. Acceptance: pushing a v* tag produces a release with downloadable installers for all three OSes; the existing CI gate matrix stays green. Test first where practical (workflow validated on a pre-release tag).
close_reason: Release workflow (release.yml) already builds per-OS installers on a v* tag and attaches them — proven on v0.4.0 (macOS dmg/app, Windows msi/exe, Linux deb/rpm/AppImage). Enhanced: macOS now builds a universal Intel+Apple Silicon binary; added RELEASING.md (tag->draft->publish flow) and fixed the stale 0.1.0 header. Universal-mac change is inspection-validated and will be exercised on the next tag. Signing/notarization tracked in l7m/dtt.

title: Encrypted password prompt never appears (window.prompt unsupported in Tauri webview)
description: openWithPassword used window.prompt to collect a PDF password, but the Tauri (WRY/WKWebView) webview returns null from window.prompt. So a password-protected PDF silently aborted: no prompt, no open. Root cause found via systematic debugging (hasXfa ruled out; only the prompt path matched the symptom). Fix: in-app password <dialog>; retry loop extracted to src/app/password.ts and tested against the real encrypted fixtures.
close_reason: Fixed in f5febfd: in-app password <dialog> replaces window.prompt (unsupported in Tauri webview). Retry loop extracted to src/app/password.ts with 4 tests vs real encrypted fixtures. Needs live re-verify in tauri dev.

title: Reopened form shows blank: existing field values not loaded into overlays
description: On load, createModel starts with empty fieldValues and the overlay controls were populated only from the model. With ENABLE_FORMS the canvas no longer paints field values either, so reopening a saved/filled PDF showed blank controls although the values are correctly stored in the file (verified by save round-trip). Fix: listFormFields captures each field's current value; the viewer shows the model edit if present, else the PDF's existing value.
close_reason: listFormFields captures current value; viewer shows edit ?? existing value. Read-back test (fill->save->reopen) green. 75 tests.

title: Form fields render twice: canvas paints widgets under the overlay
description: renderPageToCanvas used pdf.js default annotationMode (ENABLE), which paints interactive form widget appearances onto the canvas. With the HTML overlay controls on top, field values rendered twice (small canvas copy under large overlay control), found during M2 live verification. Fix: render with AnnotationMode.ENABLE_FORMS so the canvas skips form widgets.
close_reason: Fixed: render with AnnotationMode.ENABLE_FORMS so the canvas no longer paints form widgets. Gate+build green.

title: Accessibility pass: labelled overlays, keyboard operation, focus, contrast (WCAG 2.2 AA)
description:
Make the whole app operable without a mouse and legible to assistive tech, to a WCAG 2.2 AA floor.
- Every form-field overlay and annotation control has an accessible name/role; the field's PDF name feeds the label.
- Full keyboard path: open, navigate fields/annotations, edit, place/move/resize, save — all reachable via keyboard with visible focus states.
- Contrast and focus indicators meet 2.2 AA; the document canvas exposes page structure to AT where feasible.
Test first: keyboard-only walkthrough of fill+annotate+save in a test; automated a11y assertions (roles/names/contrast) on the overlay layer.
Commit: "feat(a11y): keyboard operation and labelled controls".
close_reason: Baseline AA: stamp container focusable+role+name+Delete-key delete; contrast fixes (white-on-accent to AA); focus-visible on fields/stamps; controls labelled. 2 new a11y tests. Deferred (noted): arrow-key move/resize and canvas page-structure exposure. Live SR/contrast verification at M5 gate.

title: Lazy / virtualized page rendering for large documents
description:
Replace 'render every page' (m0-6) with viewport virtualization so a several-hundred-page PDF stays responsive and bounded in memory.
- Render only pages near the viewport; recycle/destroy off-screen canvases; keep page placeholders so scroll position and the coordinate seam stay correct.
- Overlays for off-screen pages are not mounted but reattach correctly on scroll-in (no state in the overlay — model is the source of truth).
Test first: with a large fixture, only near-viewport pages hold live canvases; scrolling mounts/unmounts without losing field/annotation positions.
Commit: "perf(render): virtualize page rendering for large documents".
close_reason: Virtualized rendering: sized placeholders + IntersectionObserver render/free pages near the viewport; pageDisplaySize keeps scroll height + seam correct. Live-verified (large.pdf scroll, forms/rotated placement, zoom, annotation reattach). 6 tests (pageDisplaySize + placeholder DOM).

title: Graceful handling of corrupt, non-PDF and oversized files
description:
open_pdf + PDF.js load must fail safe, never crash the app or leave a half-rendered view.
- Non-PDF or corrupt bytes: catch the PDF.js load rejection and show a clear, dismissible error; the previous document (if any) stays intact.
- Oversized files: enforce a sane upper bound (configurable) and warn rather than freezing the webview.
Test first: loading a truncated/garbage fixture surfaces the error path and leaves app state unchanged.
Commit: "feat(io): fail gracefully on corrupt or non-pdf input".
close_reason: Size limit enforced in Rust (tested boundary); non-PDF rejected by pdf.js; failed loads keep prior render and show a status alert. Rust+frontend gates green.

title: Encrypted / password-protected PDF handling
description:
Many real-world forms are encrypted, often with an empty user password.
- Detect encryption on open. If it opens with an empty password, proceed transparently. Otherwise prompt for a password (one retry path), or refuse cleanly with a clear message.
- Save must NOT silently strip encryption or silently drop permissions. For 0.1.0 either preserve the encryption state or, if pdf-lib cannot, warn the user that the saved copy is unencrypted. Document the chosen behaviour.
Test first: an empty-user-password fixture opens and round-trips; a password-required fixture triggers the prompt/refusal path.
Commit: "feat(io): detect and handle encrypted pdfs".
close_reason: openPdfDocument handles empty-password (transparent) + password prompt/retry with typed errors; isEncryptedPdf detection; save REFUSED on encrypted docs (EncryptedSaveError) since pdf-lib can't decrypt AES — no silent stripping. 10 new tests (5 open + round-trip/detect/refuse). Chosen behaviour to be captured in m5-5 known-gaps doc.

title: Harden Tauri: CSP, capability allowlist, path scoping
description:
Lock the Tauri shell down before features land on top of it.
- Strict Content-Security-Policy for the webview (no remote origins; PDF.js worker served locally; no inline script beyond what Vite needs).
- Capability allowlist: expose ONLY the open_pdf/save_pdf/save_pdf_as commands and the dialog plugin. Nothing else from the Tauri/Rust surface is reachable from JS.
- Filesystem scope: the Rust commands read/write only the path the user picked in the native dialog. Reject any path not granted by a dialog in this session; canonicalize and reject traversal.
Test first: a Rust unit test asserts a write to a non-granted path is refused; a doc/test asserts the CSP header is present.
Commit: "chore(security): harden tauri csp, capabilities and path scope".
close_reason: CSP + freezePrototype + withGlobalTauri:false landed; capability is core-default only with no JS-exposed plugin commands; assets emit as files for a tight connect-src. Config test green; cargo build validates. Write-path guard moved to m1-8 (no save commands yet). Live render under CSP to be confirmed in tauri dev.

title: M0 — Walking skeleton
description: Prove the toolchain end to end before any features: Tauri 2 + Vite + TypeScript, PDF.js rendering with a correctly bundled worker, file open, scroll, zoom, a hardened Tauri shell, graceful failure on bad input, and green CI (lint + typecheck + test + build) on all three desktop OSes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m0-8) passes.
close_reason: Walking skeleton complete: Tauri 2 + Vite + TypeScript app opens/renders/zooms PDFs, hardened webview, green cross-platform CI.

title: Add licence, docs, linters and Vitest config
description:
Add Apache-2.0 LICENSE (copyright "The SignetPDF contributors"), README headed "SignetPDF" with a one-line description (a minimal, free, cross-platform PDF viewer for filling forms, editing text and signing) plus build/run instructions, .editorconfig, ESLint (typescript-eslint) + Prettier, a strict tsconfig with a "typecheck" script ('tsc --noEmit'), and Vitest configured headless.
Acceptance: "npm test" exits 0; "npm run typecheck" passes; "npm run lint" passes; LICENSE is Apache-2.0; README names the app SignetPDF.
Commit: "chore: add apache-2.0 licence, docs and tooling".
close_reason: Licence, README, .editorconfig, ESLint+Prettier, and headless Vitest in place. typecheck/test/lint all green.

title: Add GitHub Actions CI matrix (linux/macos/windows)
description:
CI on ubuntu/macos/windows: install, lint, typecheck ('tsc --noEmit'), "npm test", build Tauri app.
Acceptance: green on all three runners, with the typecheck step gating the build.
Commit: "chore(ci): add cross-platform build and test matrix".
close_reason: CI matrix green on ubuntu/macos/windows: lint, typecheck, Vitest, cargo fmt/clippy/test, and Tauri release build. LF enforced via .gitattributes for cross-platform prettier.

title: Rust command open_pdf returns bytes via file dialog
description:
Tauri command: native open dialog -> read chosen PDF -> return bytes.
Test first: Rust unit test that the read fn returns a fixture's contents.
Commit: "feat(io): add open_pdf tauri command".
close_reason: open_pdf command added (dialog -> bytes); read_pdf_file unit-tested (2 cargo tests pass); rustfmt + clippy clean.

title: Render user-chosen PDF: all pages with vertical scroll
description:
Wire open_pdf to PDF.js: render every page stacked vertically, scrollable.
Acceptance: a multi-page PDF shows all pages in order.
Commit: "feat(render): render all pages of opened document".
notes: Large documents: stacking every page at full render resolution will exhaust memory on long PDFs. Acceptable for the M0 skeleton, but track page virtualization (render only near-viewport pages, recycle canvases) as a known scaling limit — see m5-9.
close_reason: Open button invokes open_pdf and renders all pages stacked/scrollable via renderAllPages; fixture renders all pages on startup. Frontend gate green.

title: M0 gate: full suite green on three OSes
description:
DoD for M0 (verification gate; no commit).
- App opens, picks a PDF, renders all pages, zoom works.
- CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows (lint + typecheck + test + build).
- No PDF.js worker errors in the webview.
- Tauri hardened: CSP set, capability allowlist in place, file I/O scoped to dialog-granted paths (m0-9).
- Corrupt / non-PDF / oversized input fails gracefully without crashing (m0-10).
acceptance_criteria:
- App opens a chosen PDF, renders all pages, zoom in/out/fit work.
- CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows (lint + typecheck + test + Tauri build).
- No PDF.js worker errors in the webview.
- Tauri hardened: CSP set, capability allowlist in place, file I/O scoped to dialog-granted paths (m0-9).
- Corrupt / non-PDF / oversized input fails gracefully without crashing (m0-10).
close_reason: M0 DoD met: app opens and renders all pages of complex PDFs with working zoom (user-verified live); CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows (lint+typecheck+test+build); no worker errors under the hardened CSP; corrupt/non-PDF/oversized input handled (m0-10). Write-path scoping carried into m1-8.

title: M1 — Document model + coordinate seam + save
description: The backbone. One immutable DocumentModel as single source of truth, the user-space<->screen coordinate seam (the project's highest-risk maths), and a save projection that round-trips an empty model. Blocked until the M0 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m1-11) passes.
close_reason: Backbone complete: immutable DocumentModel + mutators, coordinate seam (cross-checked vs pdf.js + round-trip), pure save projection, Rust atomic+scoped save, save UI with dirty guard. Coverage gate + cross-OS CI green.

title: Build awkward-PDF fixture corpus
description:
/fixtures: linearised PDF, a /Rotate 90 page, an AcroForm with each field type, a no-forms PDF, one XFA PDF (for refusal tests). Document each in fixtures/README.
Commit: "test(fixtures): add awkward-pdf corpus".
notes: Also add: an encrypted PDF with an empty user password, an encrypted PDF that requires a password (for m1-12), and a several-hundred-page PDF (for m5-9 virtualization). Document each in fixtures/README.
close_reason: Corpus added: acroform (all field types), xfa (/XFA refusal), linearized, encrypted-empty + encrypted-password (qpdf AES-256), large (300pp). Verified via pdf.js/pdf-lib/qpdf; documented in fixtures/README.

title: M1 gate: coverage and round-trip green on three OSes
description:
DoD for M1 (verification gate; no commit).
- >=90% line coverage on model + coordinate modules.
- Empty-model save round-trip passes for all non-XFA fixtures.
- CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows.
- No annotation/field state in rendering code (seam intact).
acceptance_criteria:
- >=90% line coverage on the model and coordinate modules.
- Empty-model save round-trips for all non-XFA fixtures (identical page count + AcroForm field set).
- screenToModel(modelToScreen(p)) round-trip property holds across all rotations and scales.
- No annotation/field state in rendering code (seam intact).
- CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows.
close_reason: M1 DoD met: model+seam coverage 100% lines / 92.9% branches (>=90%, enforced in CI); empty-model save round-trip green for all non-XFA fixtures; CI matrix green on linux/macos/windows (coverage gate included); rendering code holds no annotation/field state. Live Save/Save As to be eyeballed in tauri dev.

title: Pure field-value mutation (immutable)
description:
setFieldValue(model, fieldName, value) -> NEW model; original untouched; dirty=true.
Test first: add/update entry without mutating input.
Commit: "feat(model): add immutable field-value mutation".
close_reason: setFieldValue immutable mutator added (add/replace, dirty, input untouched, frozen). 4 tests cover it; gate green.

title: Pure annotation mutations (add/update/remove, immutable)
description:
addAnnotation/updateAnnotation/removeAnnotation — id-based, return new model, dirty=true; ids from one central helper.
Test first: add->update->remove yields expected list; inputs never mutated.
Commit: "feat(model): add immutable annotation mutations".
close_reason: add/update/remove annotation mutators added; central createId helper; id-based, immutable, frozen. 4 mutation tests + id test; gate green (26 total).

title: Capture per-page geometry from PDF.js
description:
PageGeometry { index, width, height, rotation } in user-space units from the PDF.js page (intrinsic size + /Rotate). Populate model.pages on load.
Test first: fixture with /Rotate 90 reports correct width/height/rotation.
Commit: "feat(model): capture per-page user-space geometry".
close_reason: capturePageGeometry (unrotated size + rotation, one canonical space) and immutable withPages added; /Rotate 90 fixture verifies rotation handling. Gate green (30 tests).

title: Rust command save_pdf / save_pdf_as
description:
Write bytes to an existing path (save) and to a dialog-chosen path (save as).
Test first: Rust unit test round-trips bytes through a temp path.
Commit: "feat(io): add save_pdf and save_pdf_as commands".
notes:
Crash-safe write: write to a temp file in the same directory then atomically rename over the target, so a failed or interrupted write never corrupts the user's original PDF. fsync before rename. Refuse to write outside the dialog-granted path (ties into the Tauri capability scoping in m0-9).
Path scoping (moved here from m0-9, which hardened CSP/capabilities): save_pdf/save_pdf_as must write ONLY to a path the user granted via a dialog this session. Keep a session set of dialog-granted paths; canonicalize the target and reject traversal or any path not in that set. Test-first: a Rust unit test asserts a write to a non-granted path is refused.
close_reason: save_pdf/save_pdf_as added with atomic temp+rename writes and a dialog-granted-path guard (canonicalized); open_pdf grants opened path. 8 cargo tests (incl. refuse-non-granted); fmt+clippy clean.

title: Wire Save / Save As in UI with unsaved-changes guard
description:
Connect UI to the save projection + Rust write commands. Use model.dirty to warn on close/open with unsaved changes; clear after a successful save.
Commit: "feat(app): wire save/save-as with unsaved-changes guard".
close_reason: Save/Save As wired through saveModel + Rust save_pdf/save_pdf_as; model carried in app state; markSaved clears dirty; discard-confirm on open + beforeunload guard. markSaved unit-tested; gate+build green. Live save to verify in tauri dev.

title: M2 — Form filling (AcroForm)
description: Detect and fill existing AcroForm fields; refuse XFA gracefully. Blocked until the M1 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m2-7) passes.
close_reason: Form filling complete: AcroForm detection + XFA refusal, interactive overlays for all field types via the seam, edits bound through setFieldValue, values written back via pdf-lib with appearance regen, existing values loaded on open. User-verified live; cross-OS CI green.

title: Render interactive overlay for text fields
description:
Position editable text inputs over text fields using modelToScreen; re-position on zoom/scroll.
Test first: a field's overlay rect matches the converted user-space rect at a known scale.
Commit: "feat(forms): render text-field overlay".
close_reason: Overlay layer added (per-page container+overlay from renderAllPages); text inputs placed via fieldScreenRect (seam-based, tested at scale 2 + rot 90); re-place on zoom; fields cached on load. Gate+build green (63 tests).

title: Render checkbox and radio overlays
description:
Interactive checkbox and radio controls aligned to field rects; enforce radio-group exclusivity.
Test first: toggling a radio updates the group's selected value correctly.
Commit: "feat(forms): render checkbox and radio overlays".
close_reason: Checkbox + radio controls added to the factory; per-widget on-value captured; native radio-group exclusivity via shared name. jsdom DOM tests cover types/on-values/exclusivity. Gate green.

title: Render dropdown/choice overlays
description:
Choice (combo/list) controls populated from field options.
Test first: options parsed from the field render in order; selection captured.
Commit: "feat(forms): render choice-field overlays".
close_reason: Dropdown (<select>) and option-list (multi-row <select>) controls added, populated from field options in order. jsdom tests cover order + selection. Gate+build green (69 tests).

title: Bind field edits into the model
description:
Route every control's change through setFieldValue so the model is the single source of truth (no state in the overlay).
Test first: editing each field type updates model.fieldValues; overlay reflects model on re-render.
Commit: "feat(forms): bind field edits to document model".
close_reason: Edits routed through setFieldValue via bindFieldControl (per-kind events); applyFieldValue restores model values on re-render; controls stateless; dirty now fires. jsdom tests cover all kinds. Gate green (73).

title: M2 gate: open a form, fill all types, save, re-open
description:
DoD for M2 (verification gate; no commit).
- Every field type fillable; values persist after save+re-open.
- XFA still refused cleanly.
- CI matrix green.
acceptance_criteria:
- Every AcroForm field type (text, checkbox, radio, choice) is fillable via its overlay.
- Values persist exactly after save + re-open, visible in viewers that do not regenerate appearances.
- XFA documents are refused cleanly.
- CI matrix green.
close_reason: M2 DoD met: all field types fill and persist through save+reopen (user-verified live + golden round-trip); existing values load on reopen; XFA refused; rendering correct (ENABLE_FORMS); CI green on linux/macos/windows.

title: M3 — Free-text annotation
description: Add, edit, move, resize and delete free-text boxes; render them via the coordinate seam and bake them on save. Blocked until the M2 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m3-8) passes.
close_reason: All M3 children closed; gate m3-8 passed.

title: Edit text box content inline
description:
Select a box and edit its text; commit via updateAnnotation.
Test first: editing updates the model's text; cancel leaves it unchanged.
Commit: "feat(text): edit text box content".
close_reason: Text overlay: buildTextBoxControl + bindTextBoxControl (commit on blur/Enter, Escape cancels); wired render+edit into main.ts. Also delivered m3-1's create-tool UI (Text button + click-to-create) and font fetch on save. 7 new tests; build bundles font as 'self' asset.

title: Move text box by dragging
description:
Drag to reposition; convert deltas through the coordinate seam; update origin via updateAnnotation.
Test first: a drag of N screen px moves origin by the correct user-space delta at a given scale.
Commit: "feat(text): move text box by drag".
close_reason: moveTextBox (pure, seam-based delta) + grip-drag wiring; box overlay restructured to container+grip+textarea. 4 new tests (2 pure, 2 DOM drag).

title: Resize text box with handles
description:
Resize handles update width/height (user space); clamp to a sensible minimum.
Test first: resize updates dimensions correctly and respects the minimum.
Commit: "feat(text): resize text box".
close_reason: resizeTextBox (seam-based, rotation-correct via anchor+handle corners, min-size clamp) + resize handle wiring; shared onHandleDrag plumbing. textBoxScreenRect moved to pure transform module. 3 new tests.

title: Embed a Unicode-capable font for drawing text
description:
pdf-lib standard fonts are Latin-only; embed one open-licensed Unicode-ish font (e.g. a subset) for drawText, or document the Latin-only limitation for 0.1.0.
Test first: embedding succeeds and a non-ASCII glyph renders (or the limitation test asserts the documented fallback).
Commit: "feat(save): embed unicode font for text drawing".
close_reason: embedUnicodeFont registers fontkit + embeds Noto Sans (OFL, subset); 3 tests prove non-ASCII encodes/draws where Helvetica throws

title: M3 gate: add/edit/move/resize/delete, save, re-open
description:
DoD for M3 (verification gate; no commit).
- Text boxes across multiple pages added/edited/moved/resized/deleted.
- Saved text appears in the right places on re-open.
- CI matrix green.
acceptance_criteria:
- Free-text boxes can be added, edited, moved, resized and deleted across multiple pages.
- Saved text appears at the correct page and position on re-open.
- Non-ASCII text renders via the embedded font, or the Latin-only limitation is documented and tested.
- CI matrix green.
close_reason: M3 DoD met: add/edit/move/resize/delete across pages (97 FE + 8 Rust tests), text round-trips to correct page/position incl non-ASCII via embedded font, GUI verified live, CI matrix green on ubuntu/macos/windows (run 27557683967).

title: M4 — Visual signature
description: Draw or import a signature image, place/move/scale it, and embed it on save. Visual only — no cryptography. Blocked until the M3 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m4-6) passes.
close_reason: All M4 children closed; gate m4-6 passed.

title: Import an existing signature image
description:
Import a PNG/JPG from disk (via the open dialog) as a signature image.
Test first: a fixture image imports to bytes with the correct detected type.
Commit: "feat(sign): import signature image".
close_reason: open_image Rust command (size-limited read, no path grant) + Rust fixture test; detectImageType (pure, 3 tests) + importImageAsStamp canvas rasterise; Import action wired in the signature dialog. 9 Rust + 3 FE tests.

title: Place signature as a stamp at click point
description:
Place the image as a SignatureStamp at a clicked point (screenToModel) via addAnnotation.
Test first: placement yields a stamp with the expected page and user-space origin.
Commit: "feat(sign): place signature stamp".
close_reason: createSignatureStampAt (pure, seam-based) + stamp overlay (image render + delete) + signature dialog/pad placement wiring; annotationScreenRect shared with text. 4 new tests.

title: Move and scale stamp preserving aspect ratio
description:
Drag to move; corner-scale preserving aspect ratio; update via updateAnnotation. Aspect maths in one tested helper.
Test first: scaling keeps width/height ratio within epsilon; move applies correct user-space delta.
Commit: "feat(sign): move and scale signature stamp".
close_reason: moveStamp + scaleStamp (aspect-locked, seam-based) wired to grip/handle; shared onHandleDrag extracted to annotations/drag.ts. 5 new tests (pure move/scale incl ratio + clamp, DOM move/scale).

title: M4 gate: draw/import, place, move/scale, save, re-open
description:
DoD for M4 (verification gate; no commit).
- Draw or import a signature, place/move/scale, save, re-open -> appears correctly.
- CI matrix green.
acceptance_criteria:
- A signature can be drawn or imported, placed, moved and scaled (aspect preserved).
- Saved signature appears at the correct page, position and size on re-open.
- CI matrix green.
close_reason: M4 DoD met: draw/import signature, place, move, scale (aspect preserved), delete, save, re-open verified live; save golden asserts page/position/size; CI matrix green on ubuntu/macos/windows (run 27559660797).

title: M5 — Polish and first release
description: Flatten-on-export, undo/redo, keyboard shortcuts, cross-platform packaging and a 0.1.0 release. Binary code-signing/notarisation is explicitly out of scope and documented as a known gap. Blocked until the M4 gate closes.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks closed and the milestone gate (pdfv-m5-7) passes.
close_reason: All M5 children closed; gate m5-7 passed; 0.1.0 released.

title: Flatten-on-export option
description:
Add an export mode that bakes filled fields and annotations into static page content with no editable layer (pdf-lib form.flatten() plus the already-drawn annotation content). Keep non-flattened save as the default.
Test first (golden-file): flattened output preserves the visual content but exposes no editable AcroForm fields; non-flattened save still round-trips editable.
Commit: "feat(save): add flatten-on-export option".
close_reason: saveModel flatten option (form.flatten after appearances) + Export flat action (Save As, leaves working doc editable). Golden tests: flattened removes editable fields + bakes value into text; non-flatten stays editable. 124 FE.

title: Undo/redo over the document model
description:
A history stack of immutable model snapshots (cheap given the immutable mutators). Undo/redo across field edits and annotation add/edit/move/resize/delete; a new action after undo clears the redo branch; dirty flag stays consistent.
Test first: undo restores the prior model; redo reapplies; branching clears redo.
Commit: "feat(app): add undo/redo over document model".
notes: Memory: sourceBytes is immutable and can be tens of MB. History snapshots must share it by reference, never copy — snapshot only the small mutable parts (fieldValues, annotations, dirty). Bound the history depth (e.g. 100 steps) so a long session cannot grow unbounded.
close_reason: Pure history module (createHistory/record/undo/redo/replacePresent, 100-step cap, shares sourceBytes by reference) + main.ts integration (applyEdit on all mutations, Undo/Redo buttons, save replaces present). 7 history tests; 122 FE total. Live verification at M5 gate.

title: Cross-platform packaging
description:
Configure Tauri bundling to produce installers/artefacts for Linux, macOS and Windows; build them in CI.
Acceptance: an installable artefact per OS is produced by the workflow.
Commit: "chore(release): configure cross-platform bundling".
close_reason: Release workflow (tauri-action) builds installers per OS on a v* tag and attaches to a draft release; bundling configured (targets all, icons). macOS bundle verified locally (.app + .dmg). Per-OS artefacts produced when the workflow runs at release time (m5-6).

title: Cut 0.1.0 release with built artefacts
description:
Tag v0.1.0 and publish a GitHub release for SignetPDF, attaching the per-OS artefacts from the bundling workflow. Follow conventional-commit/tag conventions.
Commit/tag: "chore(release): v0.1.0".
close_reason: v0.1.0 tagged; release workflow built per-OS installers (deb/AppImage/rpm, dmg/app, exe/msi); release published (not draft).

title: M5 gate: installable build per OS, flatten + undo/redo work
description:
DoD for M5 (verification gate; no commit).
- Installable build per platform; flatten-on-export works; undo/redo works.
- 0.1.0 release published with artefacts.
- Known gaps documented.
acceptance_criteria:
- Installable build produced per platform (linux/macos/windows).
- Flatten-on-export works; undo/redo works; keyboard shortcuts work.
- Accessibility floor met (WCAG 2.2 AA) and large documents stay responsive (virtualized rendering).
- 0.1.0 release published with per-OS artefacts; known gaps documented.
close_reason: M5 DoD met: installable build per OS (published v0.1.0 artefacts), flatten + undo/redo + keyboard verified, a11y AA baseline, virtualized rendering live-verified, known gaps documented, CI matrix green.

title: Markup annotations: freehand ink, sticky-note comments, and shapes
description:
Extend annotations beyond text boxes and signatures with three new pointer-drawn markup types: freehand ink (pen), anchored sticky-note comments (icon + popup text), and shapes (rectangle, ellipse, line/arrow). Each is a first-class annotation a user can create, move, resize, and delete.

Design: add new Annotation kinds (e.g. 'ink', 'note', 'shape') to the discriminated union (invariant 1) holding page and all geometry in PDF user space (points, bottom-left origin) — ink as point paths, note as an anchor point + comment string, shapes as a bounding box plus shape kind/stroke/fill. Route every change through the central immutable model mutators (addAnnotation/updateAnnotation/removeAnnotation) so undo/redo and dirty-tracking come for free, and reuse the coordinate seam (invariant 2) for both hit-testing/drawing and screen<->model conversion — rendering holds no annotation state. Save stays a pure projection (invariant 3): map each kind to pdf-lib (ink paths via drawing ops or an ink annotation, text/popup annotation for notes, rect/ellipse/line draw ops for shapes) and prove it with golden round-trip tests. Respect existing keyboard move/resize + snapping and the keyboard-creation gap tracked in SignetPDF-18t.

Acceptance: a user can draw freehand ink, drop a sticky note with comment text, and draw rectangle/ellipse/line-or-arrow shapes; each is selectable, movable, resizable (where applicable), and deletable; all persist through save/re-open (golden round-trip tests) and through undo/redo; geometry is correct across rotations and scales.

title: Text markup: highlight, underline, strikethrough on selected text
description:
Add PDF text-markup annotations driven from the existing text selection. Three markup styles over a selected run of text: highlight (colored marker), underline, and strikethrough, with a user-chosen color. This is distinct from the existing free TextBox/SignatureStamp annotations — markup is anchored to selected glyphs, not free-placed.

Design: introduce a new Annotation kind (e.g. 'markup') in the discriminated union (DocumentModel, invariant 1) carrying page, the markup style, color, and the quad geometry in PDF user space (points, bottom-left origin) — never screen pixels. Reuse the coordinate seam (invariant 2) to map the selection rects to user-space quads and to paint the overlay; rendering stays state-free. Save is a pure projection (invariant 3): emit pdf-lib highlight/underline/strikeout markup (QuadPoints) and round-trip test it. Wire through the central model mutators so undo/redo (snapshot stack) covers it for free. Hook creation off the existing selection + right-click menu (see contextmenu.ts) and keep the WKWebView selection-repaint caveats in mind.

Acceptance: a user can highlight, underline, or strikethrough selected text and pick the color; the markup persists through save and re-open (golden round-trip test); undo/redo works; coordinates correct across rotations (0/90/180/270) and scales.

title: Keyboard-only annotation creation and placement
description: Today a text box or signature is created/placed by pointer (click on page, or right-click menu); once placed it is fully keyboard operable. Add a keyboard path to create and position a new annotation without a pointer (WCAG 2.2 / documented a11y gap in KNOWN_ISSUES). Acceptance: a user can create a text box and place a signature using only the keyboard; KNOWN_ISSUES updated.

title: Windows code-signing for release builds
description: Windows builds are unsigned, so SmartScreen warns (documented in KNOWN_ISSUES). Sign the installer/exe in the release workflow with a code-signing certificate (secret). Acceptance: a downloaded installer runs without the SmartScreen 'unknown publisher' warning (reputation permitting); KNOWN_ISSUES updated. Depends on the release build workflow.

title: macOS notarization for release builds
description: macOS bundles are unnotarized, so Gatekeeper warns on first launch (documented in KNOWN_ISSUES). Sign with a Developer ID certificate and notarize/staple in the release workflow. Needs an Apple Developer account and secrets (cert, app-specific password / API key). Acceptance: a downloaded .dmg opens without the Gatekeeper 'unidentified developer' block; KNOWN_ISSUES updated. Depends on the release build workflow.

title: WKWebView leaves stale search highlights painted after registry cleared
description: Console from real WKWebView with a no-match query: CSS.highlights is empty (registryKeys: [], both sets absent) and count reads 'No results', yet the highlight paint stays on screen. WKWebView does not invalidate ::highlight() pseudos on CSS.highlights.delete(); Chromium and Playwright's WebKit do, so the existing Vitest/Playwright coverage can't reproduce it. Fix: force the live text layers to repaint after refreshSearchHighlights mutates the registry.
close_reason: Forced text-layer repaint after highlight registry changes; confirmed fixed in real WKWebView

title: Phase D: roving-tabindex toolbar, overflow menu, Sign aria-pressed
description: WAI-ARIA toolbar roving tabindex (Left/Right/Home/End, skip disabled; pure helper TDD); responsive overflow 'More' menu for Save As + Export; Sign aria-pressed parity with Text.
close_reason: Roving toolbar + overflow menu + Sign aria-pressed; roving/dock tests, 184 green

title: Phase C: page indicator + loading state
description: Live 'n / total' page indicator from IntersectionObserver ratios (pure mostVisiblePage helper, TDD); accessible loading spinner (aria-busy) while a document opens/renders.
close_reason: Page indicator + loading state; mostVisiblePage TDD, 176 tests green

title: Phase B: Escape-cancel, armed hints, drag-and-drop open
description: Escape disarms Text/Sign; info toast hint on arming; drag-and-drop a PDF to open (Rust read_pdf command + onDragDropEvent + shared openBytes refactor + drag-over highlight).
close_reason: Drag-drop (Rust-owned, secure) + Esc cancel + arm hints; 11 cargo tests, 173 JS tests, typecheck+lint green

title: 0.3.0 UI/UX: zoom, interactions, navigation, a11y
description: Post-0.2.0 UX pass: zoom ladder/reset/pinch, Escape-to-cancel + armed-tool hints + drag-and-drop open, page indicator + loading state, roving-tabindex toolbar + responsive overflow menu + Sign aria-pressed. Presentation/input only plus one read-only Rust read_pdf command. Per the approved plan.
close_reason: All four phases complete

title: Phase A: zoom ladder, reset-to-100%, pinch/Ctrl-wheel
description: Replace multiplicative ZOOM_STEP with a preset ladder (stepZoom); add zoomByDelta for continuous pinch/Ctrl-wheel zoom anchored to cursor; reset-to-100% via zoom readout click + Cmd/Ctrl+0 (new zoom-reset shortcut). TDD zoom.ts + shortcuts.ts.
close_reason: Zoom ladder + reset + pinch/Ctrl-wheel; 17 zoom/shortcut tests, full suite 173 green

title: Dialog + overlay token alignment
description: Re-point password/signature dialogs and overlay chrome (text box, stamp, fields) at tokens and shared .btn classes; standardize focus rings. Depends on tokens. Per §4,§6.
close_reason: Implemented per docs/ui-redesign.md; typecheck, lint, 166 tests, and vite build all green

title: Pretty empty-state placeholder on startup
description: Stop loading the bundled fixture on launch. Show a centered placeholder (wordmark/icon, 'Open a PDF to get started', Open button wired to openUserPdf) when no document is loaded; hide once a doc opens. Per §9.
close_reason: Implemented per docs/ui-redesign.md; typecheck, lint, 166 tests, and vite build all green

title: Floating toast/status UI integration
description: Replace #status p with #toasts live-region container; swap setStatus for notify(variant); update all call sites (Saved/Exported=success, failures=error, encrypted notice=info). Depends on tokens+toast manager. Per §3.
close_reason: Implemented per docs/ui-redesign.md; typecheck, lint, 166 tests, and vite build all green

title: Floating bottom dock toolbar
description: Restructure index.html header into nav.dock role=toolbar with grouped icon buttons; styles.css .dock; main.ts wiring incl. Sign armed state + Save dirty dot + overflow More. Depends on tokens+icons. Per §2.
close_reason: Implemented per docs/ui-redesign.md; typecheck, lint, 166 tests, and vite build all green

title: Headless toast manager
description: New src/app/toast.ts: create/dismiss/auto-timeout/live-region routing (polite vs assertive by variant), max-3 stack. Headless + unit-tested first (TDD). Per §3.
close_reason: Implemented per docs/ui-redesign.md; typecheck, lint, 166 tests, and vite build all green

title: Inline Lucide icon set + icon-button helper
description: New src/app/icons.ts: CSP-safe inline SVG strings keyed by name (folder-open, save, copy, layers, type, pen-tool, undo-2, redo-2, minus, plus, maximize-2, more-horizontal, info, check-circle, alert-triangle) and iconButton(name,label,id) helper. Unit-tested. Per §2.
close_reason: Implemented per docs/ui-redesign.md; typecheck, lint, 166 tests, and vite build all green

title: Design tokens + shared button classes
description: Add :root token layer (color/space/radius/shadow/motion) and .btn/.btn-icon/.btn-primary/.btn-success/.btn-ghost/.sr-only utilities in styles.css. One source of truth for the redesign. Per docs/ui-redesign.md §1,§5.
close_reason: Implemented per docs/ui-redesign.md; typecheck, lint, 166 tests, and vite build all green

title: UI redesign: floating dock, toasts, empty state
description: Post-0.1.0 UI polish per docs/ui-redesign.md. Bottom-center floating dock toolbar with inline-SVG icon buttons, floating centered toast system (info/success/error), shared design tokens + button classes, dialog/overlay token alignment, and a pretty empty-state placeholder shown on startup instead of the bundled fixture.
close_reason: All child tasks complete

title: Bump CI/release actions off deprecated Node 20 (checkout, mise-action)
description: actions/checkout@v4 and jdx/mise-action@v2 run on Node 20, which GitHub forces to Node 24 from 2026-06-16 and removes 2026-09-16. Bump to Node 24 majors (checkout@v5, mise-action@v4) in ci.yml and release.yml.
close_reason: Bumped checkout@v4->v5 and mise-action@v2->v4 (Node 24) in ci.yml + release.yml (6ac0c2c). rust-cache/tauri-action not flagged.

title: Zoom in/out/fit controls
description:
Zoom in/out and fit-to-width, re-rendering at the chosen scale.
Commit: "feat(render): add zoom controls".
close_reason: Zoom in/out/fit-width re-render at scale; pure zoom module unit-tested (clamp + fit). Frontend gate green.

title: Delete selected text box
description:
Delete via key/button using removeAnnotation.
Test first: delete removes exactly the selected annotation.
Commit: "feat(text): delete selected text box".
close_reason: Delete button per box (accessible <button>, aria-label) wired to removeAnnotation; 1 DOM test asserts exactly the selected id is removed.

title: Keyboard shortcuts
description:
Shortcuts for open, save, save-as, undo, redo and zoom, using platform-appropriate modifiers (Cmd on macOS, Ctrl elsewhere).
Test first: a dispatched shortcut maps to the correct action; modifier resolves per platform.
Commit: "feat(app): add keyboard shortcuts".
close_reason: Pure matchShortcut (platform modifier resolution) + detectPlatform; window keydown wired to open/save/save-as/undo/redo/zoom. 5 tests.

title: Document known gaps (incl. binary signing)
description:
README/KNOWN_ISSUES: binary code-signing is out of scope for 0.1.0 — macOS builds are unnotarised (Gatekeeper warning) and Windows builds are unsigned (SmartScreen warning); note the workarounds for users. Record any font/XFA limitations too.
Commit: "docs: document known gaps for 0.1.0".
notes: Also document the encrypted-PDF behaviour chosen in m1-12 (e.g. saved copies may be unencrypted) and the large-document/virtualization limits.
close_reason: Added KNOWN_ISSUES.md (unsigned binaries + workarounds, encrypted-save refusal, XFA, font coverage, no virtualization, a11y baseline/deferred); linked from README; refreshed status. Captures the m1-12 chosen behaviour.

title: Bump glib to >=0.20 once Tauri upgrades gtk-rs (GHSA-wrw7-89jp-8q8g)
description: Dependabot alert #1: glib 0.18.5 has a medium unsoundness in VariantStrIter iterator impls (patched in 0.20.0). It is a transitive, Linux-only dep capped by Tauri's gtk-rs 0.18 stack (gtk 0.18 requires glib ^0.18), so it can't be bumped today — cargo update fails. The alert was dismissed as tolerable_risk (not reachable from app code; macOS/Windows don't use glib). Action: when Tauri ships a gtk-rs 0.20 upgrade, update Tauri, confirm glib >= 0.20 in Cargo.lock, run cargo audit/build, and the alert should auto-resolve. Acceptance: glib >= 0.20.0 in src-tauri/Cargo.lock.

title: Configure the beads Dolt remote for cross-machine sync
description: Issues currently sync only via the git-tracked .beads/issues.jsonl export; the Dolt remote isn't configured, so 'bd dolt push/pull' can't run (bd warns to add it). Run 'bd dolt remote add origin git+https://github.com/acornelissen/signetpdf.git' and push, so the issue DB syncs cross-machine. Acceptance: 'bd dolt push' succeeds and a fresh clone can 'bd dolt pull'.

title: Expose page text as a semantic reading order for assistive tech
description: The selectable text layer puts real DOM text on the page, but it is positioned for selection, not structured as a reading order for screen readers (documented a11y gap). Investigate exposing an ordered, readable text structure to AT. Acceptance: a screen reader can read the page content in a sensible order; KNOWN_ISSUES updated.

title: Allow saving encrypted PDFs
description: Saving an encrypted PDF is disabled because pdf-lib cannot decrypt content; SignetPDF refuses rather than emit a broken/unencrypted file (documented limitation). Investigate a path to save edits to an encrypted source (e.g. decrypt-in-memory then re-encrypt, or an alternate writer). Acceptance: an encrypted PDF that opened with a password can be edited and saved without corrupting it; KNOWN_ISSUES updated.

title: CJK / non-Latin coverage in drawn text
description: Drawn free text embeds Noto Sans/Serif/Mono (Latin and common scripts). Characters outside that coverage (e.g. CJK) may not render in the saved PDF (documented limitation). Add broader font coverage or on-demand subsetting for non-Latin scripts. Acceptance: a saved annotation containing CJK renders correctly; KNOWN_ISSUES updated.

title: Font family selection for text annotations
description: Follow-up to SignetPDF-8ho: add a font-family picker to the text-formatting toolbar (e.g. sans/serif/mono). Requires bundling additional families, each with regular/bold/italic/bold-italic variants, and extending embedTextFonts to key on family as well as weight/style. Extend the TextBox model with a family field (default the current Noto Sans). Deferred from 8ho as the asset-heavy, lower-value part.
close_reason: Family field on TextBox + toolbar picker; embedTextFonts keyed by family (sans/serif/mono) with family->sans and per-variant fallback; save selects the family face; on-screen generic CSS mapping; main bundles 10 faces. Unit + serif save + family e2e green.

title: Snapping for text/signature insert boxes (grid or element-edge)
description: Follow-up to SignetPDF-jgl: add optional snapping during drag/resize of text boxes and signature stamps — grid snap and/or snap to other annotation edges. Keep geometry through the coordinate seam and changes through model mutators. Deferred from jgl as YAGNI for the first polish pass.
close_reason: Optional snapping for text/stamp drag, resize and scale. New pure annotations/snap.ts (grid 10pt + neighbour-edge alignment; edges take precedence over grid; 6pt threshold), unit-tested. transform.ts adapters map annotation<->Box. Overlay bindings snap the committed result unless Alt is held (bypass); siblings plumbed from main.ts. drag.ts onDone now passes the pointer-up event for modifier reads. dom-tested for grid snap, edge snap and Alt bypass on both text and stamps.

title: Context-menu Copy: replace execCommand with a real clipboard write
description: The right-click Copy action in main.ts uses the deprecated document.execCommand('copy'), which copies the live selection and only works while the selection still exists and is focused. Replace it with an explicit clipboard write of the text captured at right-click time (navigator.clipboard.writeText, or the Tauri clipboard plugin for reliability inside WKWebView). Capturing the text at menu-open time also decouples Copy from selection/focus state. Relates to the selection-highlight bug.
close_reason: Copy now writes via tauri-plugin-clipboard-manager writeText; text captured at right-click time; write-only capability. e2e asserts the selected text is written. Rust builds, all gates green.

title: Manage saved signature images
description: A small manager for saved signatures: list, preview, rename, set a default, and delete. Builds on saving imported/drawn signatures (depends on that feature). Rust owns the storage and deletion; the UI is a dialog or panel consistent with the rest of the app. Roadmap item.
close_reason: Saved-signature manager: list/preview/rename/set-default/delete. Rust owns storage+deletion via index.json sidecar (id validated as 32-hex to block path traversal) + rename/set_default/delete commands, unit-tested. New sign/manager.ts builds per-signature cards (inline rename, confirm-to-delete, set-default) wired into the signature dialog strip; dom-tested. Default sorts first.

title: Polish the text and signature insert boxes (UX)
description: Refine the in-place editing chrome for text boxes and signature stamps: cleaner grips/handles, clearer selected vs editing states, smoother drag/resize (snapping, min sizes, keyboard nudge/resize), better hit targets, hover/focus affordances, and consistent styling via the design tokens. Keep all geometry going through the coordinate seam and every change through a model mutator. Pairs with SignetPDF-8ho (floating text-formatting toolbar). Roadmap item.
close_reason: Keyboard move/resize for selected text boxes and stamps (seam + grow helpers, clamped, live reposition without re-render), selected-vs-editing state with Escape stepping back, and chrome polish (selected ring, editing border, hover, bigger hit targets). Unit + WebKit/Chromium e2e. Snapping split to SignetPDF-5dk.

title: Save imported signature images
description: Persist a signature the user draws or imports so it can be reused across sessions instead of recreating it each time. Store the PNG bytes in app data via a Rust command (file I/O stays Rust-side, matching the open/save model); keep it local-only and consider that signatures are sensitive. Surfaces in the signature dialog as 'saved signatures' to pick from. Roadmap item.
close_reason: Signatures persist via save_signature (opt-in checkbox) into app_local_data_dir; dialog lists saved thumbnails to pick and place. Rust helpers + JS store unit-tested; full flow e2e on WebKit+Chromium.

title: Floating text-formatting toolbar for text annotations
description: When a text box is selected or being edited, show a small floating toolbar near it for formatting: font size, and likely font family, bold/italic, color, and alignment. Must route every change through a model mutator (updateAnnotation) so the immutable DocumentModel stays the source of truth and undo/redo just works; the control holds no state of its own. Position via the coordinate seam (modelToScreen) so it tracks the box across scroll/zoom and the floating dock. The save projection (pdf-lib drawText) must honour the new formatting attributes, which means extending the TextBox annotation type (fontSize exists; add family/weight/style/color/align) and the embedded-font handling. Keep it accessible: keyboard reachable, labelled controls, visible focus. Roadmap item; pairs with the existing Text tool.
notes: Scope decided: size + bold/italic + color + align; font family deferred to a follow-up. BLOCKED on assets: needs NotoSans-Bold.ttf, NotoSans-Italic.ttf, NotoSans-BoldItalic.ttf (OFL) added to src/assets/fonts/ — cannot be fetched from this sandbox (network returns HTML/text for binaries). Once present: extend TextBox (bold/italic/color/align), embed 4 variants in font.ts + select per box in save drawTextBox (color via rgb, alignment via per-line width), reflect on the textarea, and add the floating toolbar (updateAnnotation-routed, seam-positioned, accessible).
close_reason: Formatting toolbar (size/bold/italic/color/align) shown on focus, state-free, routed through updateAnnotation with live textarea updates and no re-render. Model extended; save embeds all 4 Noto variants + applies color and per-line alignment. Unit + WebKit/Chromium e2e green.

title: Text search within the document
description: Find text in the open PDF and jump between matches. Depends on a pdf.js text layer (see SignetPDF-q95 selectable text), since both need extracted page text. Scope: a search field (likely a dock control or Cmd/Ctrl+F overlay), match highlighting over the page, next/previous navigation with a match count, and scroll-to-match that plays nicely with the virtualized page rendering (matches on not-yet-mounted pages must still be locatable). Respect the coordinate seam for highlight placement. Roadmap item.
close_reason: Find-in-document: Cmd/Ctrl+F bar, streamTextContent index, pure tested match logic, Custom Highlight API highlighting with current-match emphasis + next/prev nav. 10 unit + 4 e2e (WebKit+Chromium), all gates green. Pushed.

title: Selectable text with copy to clipboard
description: Render a pdf.js text layer over each page canvas so users can select text and copy it (Cmd/Ctrl+C). Must respect the coordinate seam, not paint visible glyphs (selection layer is transparent), and not interfere with the form/annotation overlays or the create-tool click targets. Consider per-page text-layer build on mount/unmount to keep large docs bounded, matching the virtualized render. Roadmap item.
close_reason: pdf.js text layer over each page with native copy; virtualized build/cancel, zoom re-render, pointer-events layering, vendored CSS + visible selection. typecheck/lint/184 tests/build green. Visual selection behaviour to be verified in tauri dev.

title: Custom right-click context menu
description: Replace the webview's default context menu with an app-specific one (e.g. copy, zoom, fit, page actions), or suppress it where it makes no sense. Note: the 'Inspect Element' entry is only present in debug builds (Tauri auto-enables the web inspector in dev); release builds don't show it, so this is about intentional right-click UX, not removing devtools. Roadmap item, deferred from the 0.3.0 UI pass.
close_reason: Custom context-sensitive right-click menu: selection/page/annotation menus, editable inputs keep native menu, placement at click point, signature threaded through dialog. Unit + WebKit/Chromium e2e coverage.
